description_long: This object consists of two tomahawk-style axes crossed together, each featuring a dark metal blade with engraved or painted symbols and patterns. The handles are wrapped in various materials, including leather and cord, and adorned with decorative elements such as beads, feathers, and small trinkets. The blades are broad and slightly curved, with one axe displaying a heart-shaped motif and the other featuring tribal or symbolic markings. The handles are cylindrical, mostly straight, and show a mix of wood and leather textures, with some sections reinforced by wrappings. Both axes have a rugged, handcrafted appearance, suggesting cultural or ceremonial significance in addition to practical use. The overall look is detailed and visually striking, with a blend of earthy tones and metallic highlights.
description: A pair of crossed tomahawk axes with decorated metal blades, leather-wrapped wooden handles, and ornamental feathers and beads.
description_view_0: Side view showing the thickness of the handles and the profile of the blades, with decorative wrappings and feathers visible.
description_view_1: Front view displaying both axe blades with engraved symbols and heart motif, as well as the full decorative detail on the handles.
description_view_2: Opposite side view highlighting the cylindrical handles, wrappings, and the attachment of feathers and beads.
description_view_3: Rear angled view showing the backs of the blades, handle wrappings, and the arrangement of decorative elements.
attribute_handle_type: Cylindrical, mostly straight wooden handles wrapped with leather and cord, featuring grip-enhancing textures and decorative elements.
attribute_blade_design: Broad, slightly curved steel blades with engraved or painted symbols, single cutting edge, and decorative motifs including a heart and tribal patterns.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with visible wrappings and possible pinning for reinforcement.
